--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Add tags to models; filter kanban AI panel selector by `kanban` tag
+---
+## What
+
+Give model configs an optional `tags` list so we can control where each model surfaces, and use a `kanban` tag to filter which models appear in the kanban app's AI panel model selector.
+
+Today the AI panel shows a synthesized Claude Code entry plus *every* `llama-agent` built-in/project/user model (`apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs::ai_list_models`). We want the panel to show only models explicitly opted in with a `kanban` tag, so the built-in model set can grow (CLI-only / embedding-adjacent models) without cluttering the panel.
+
+### 1. Parse and carry tags — `crates/swissarmyhammer-config/src/model.rs`
+
+- Add `tags: Vec<String>` to `ModelInfo` (the config one at `model.rs:741`, **not** the statusline `ModelInfo`). Annotate the serde so existing `model list --format json/yaml` output stays sane and round-trips: `#[serde(default)] pub tags: Vec<String>`.
+- Add a `pub fn parse_model_tags(content: &str) -> Vec<String>` mirroring `parse_model_description` — read a `tags:` sequence from the YAML frontmatter (the `---`…`---` block). Reuse/extend the existing `extract_yaml_frontmatter_field` helper machinery; tags is a YAML list (`tags: [kanban, cli]` or block form), not a scalar, so add a list-aware extractor. Missing/empty frontmatter → empty `Vec`.
+- Populate `tags` at both construction sites: `load_builtin_models` (`model.rs:~1063`) and `validate_and_create_model_info` (`model.rs:~1368`).
+- Update the in-crate test `ModelInfo { … }` literals (`model.rs` ~2399, 2417, 2424, 2431, 2444) and the CLI test literals in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/model/display.rs` (~84, 93, 102, 337) to set `tags` (e.g. `tags: vec![]`).
+
+### 2. Tag the built-in model YAML frontmatter — `builtin/models/*.yaml`
+
+Add a `tags:` line to the frontmatter of each built-in model. Tag with `kanban` exactly the chat models that should appear in the AI panel selector — at minimum `claude-code.yaml`, and the `llama-agent` chat models (`qwen.yaml`, `GLM.yaml`, `qwen-coder.yaml`, `qwen-moe.yaml`, `MiniMax-2.5.yaml`). Do **not** tag the embedding models (`qwen-embedding.yaml`, `nomic-embed-code.yaml`) or the tiny test model (`qwen-0.6b-test.yaml`) with `kanban`. Example frontmatter:
+
+```yaml
+---
+description: "Qwen3.6"
+tags: [kanban]
+---
+```
+
+### 3. Filter the AI panel selector — `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs::ai_list_models`
+
+- A model appears in the selector **iff** its config carries the `kanban` tag. Apply this uniformly:
+  - For the synthesized Claude Code entry, gate its inclusion on the built-in `claude-code` agent carrying the `kanban` tag (look it up via `ModelManager` rather than always pushing it). Keep the existing live-CLI-detection `available`/`hint` behavior for the entry when it is included.
+  - In the `llama-agent` loop, additionally require `agent.tags.contains("kanban")` before pushing `local_llama_model(agent)`.
+- Keep the existing executor-type gate (embedding executors stay excluded regardless of tag).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `ModelInfo` has a `tags: Vec<String>` field populated from YAML frontmatter for built-in, project, gitroot, and user models.
+- [ ] `parse_model_tags("---\ntags: [kanban, cli]\n---\n…")` returns `["kanban", "cli"]`; content with no frontmatter or no `tags:` returns `[]`.
+- [ ] Built-in chat models intended for the panel (incl. `claude-code`) carry `kanban` in their frontmatter; embedding and test models do not.
+- [ ] `ai_list_models()` returns only models whose config has the `kanban` tag — embedding models and any untagged llama model are excluded; the Claude Code entry appears only when the `claude-code` config is `kanban`-tagged (and still reflects CLI detection in `available`/`hint`).
+- [ ] `model list` CLI output (json/yaml/table) still works; tags are included in structured output.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Unit test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-config/src/model.rs` `tests` module: `parse_model_tags` for list form, block-sequence form, missing-frontmatter, and no-`tags`-key cases.
+- [ ] Unit test in the same module asserting `load_builtin_models()` yields a model with `kanban` in `tags` (e.g. `qwen`) and an embedding model without it (e.g. `qwen-embedding`).
+- [ ] Update/extend tests in `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs` (`list_models_*` tests near `model.rs:534`–`603`): assert the returned set is exactly the `kanban`-tagged models, that an untagged llama model is excluded, and that the Claude Code entry presence follows the `claude-code` tag. Reuse the existing `CLAUDE_CLI` env override for detection-dependent assertions.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-config model` passes.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app ai::models` (or the crate's model-test path) passes.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli model` passes (model list display tests still green with the new field).
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first (start with `parse_model_tags` and the `ai_list_models` filter), then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+## Review Findings (2026-05-25 12:42)
+
+### Warnings

A field-level comment that asserts the opposite of the actual behavior will mislead the next reader. Update it to: Claude Code is always `true` (the CLI is spawned lazily at use time); local llama models are always `true` (weights fetched lazily).
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs:101-102` — The `Model::hint` field doc is stale for the Claude Code case. Reword to describe the current contract: the resolved CLI path for Claude Code (when detected), or a model description for local llama entries.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-config/src/model.rs:787-800` — `extract_yaml_frontmatter_list` silently drops a scalar `tags:` value. A config that writes `tags: kanban` (a bare string instead of a sequence) parses to a YAML scalar, `as_sequence()` returns `None`, and the whole key is discarded → empty `Vec`, so the model silently never surfaces in the panel. This is consistent with the lenient parsing style and the `_list` naming, so it is acceptable as-is; consider either accepting a lone scalar as a single-element list or documenting on `parse_model_tags` that `tags` must be a YAML sequence.
